summary: Frozen HEAD 5ade483c preserves the reviewed runtime contract: gate_evidence and release_risk use content_surface by default, unchanged surfaces survive unrelated HEAD movement, affected or unresolved surfaces fail closed, and dirty fingerprint, inspection, provenance, lifecycle, legacy merge-delta, and explicit strict_head guarantees remain intact.
inspection_summary: Independently inspected the current review request, full origin/main change surface, 6ed774dc-to-5ade483c delta, agent-review and content-binding control flow, Story/Architecture/Spec, current-HEAD verification evidence, gate result, and active authorized lifecycle. The only post-6ed774dc tracked change is the Story-local review-budget amendment; runtime source/spec/test bytes are unchanged.
inspection_evidence: .vibepro/pr/story-vibepro-surface-aware-agent-review-freshness/verification-evidence.json records current frozen HEAD 5ade483c unit 39/39 and E2E 8/8; .vibepro/reviews/story-vibepro-surface-aware-agent-review-freshness/gate/review-result-gate_evidence.json independently records a current-HEAD content_surface pass.
judgment_delta: Initial concern that the new HEAD invalidated the prior runtime conclusion -> resolved because the only delta is a bounded Story-local max_subagent_count and rationale update; reviewed runtime source, Spec, and tests are byte-identical to the independently passed 6ed774dc surface.

Initial concern that current verification might still describe the prior HEAD -> resolved because focused 39/39 and E2E 8/8 entries are strict-head bound to 5ade483c with clean user and raw dirty fingerprints.

Initial concern that content_surface defaults could weaken fail-closed behavior -> resolved by the unchanged build/evaluateContentBinding and bindReviewResult order: missing, unbound, unreadable, hash mismatch, and dirty-fingerprint mismatch stale before reuse.

Initial concern that #381 legacy reuse or strict compatibility could be bypassed -> resolved because content-bound current results use hashes, unbound legacy HEAD changes reach evaluateMergeDeltaReviewReuse and fail closed on unresolved diffs or impacted files, while reasoned role and CLI strict_head still stale on any HEAD mismatch.

Initial concern that verification success could mask provenance or lifecycle regression -> resolved by current focused evidence covering inspection-first and lifecycle tests, the active lifecycle matching authorization 6b8617f3-24a0-47b5-a6d2-0ad27cc85a65, and the independent gate result retaining content_surface inspection inputs. The gate verdict corroborates but does not replace this runtime review.
